message:
test(harness): emit SCENARIO_RESULT markers for btrfs rw xattr E2E coverage (bd-b25w4)

bd-b25w4's acceptance criteria calls for 'SCENARIO_RESULT markers in
logs prove coverage in CI artifacts'.  Eight btrfs rw xattr E2E tests
already existed in crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs (set/get/list/
remove, multiple attrs, directory xattrs, XATTR_CREATE → EEXIST,
XATTR_REPLACE → ENODATA, overwrite, get-nonexistent, remove-nonexistent)
but none emitted the SCENARIO_RESULT|scenario_id=...|outcome=...|detail=
CI-visible marker.  Without that line, a passing test is indistinguishable
in CI artifacts from a test that soft-skipped (returned early because
btrfs_fuse_available() was false).

Thread an `emit_scenario_result(..., "PASS", Some(<detail>))` through
each of the eight tests, using the `btrfs_rw_xattr_<name>` scenario_id
convention consistent with the ext4 rw counterparts' naming.  Detail
strings pin the specific invariant each test proves:

  - set_get_list_remove            → user_namespace_full_cycle
  - multiple_attributes            → three_distinct_user_xattrs
  - on_directory                   → user_xattr_on_directory_inode
  - create_existing_reports_eexist → errno=EEXIST_with_no_side_effects
  - replace_missing_reports_enodata→ errno=ENODATA_with_no_side_effects
  - overwrite_value                → second_setxattr_updates_value
  - get_nonexistent_returns_error  → getxattr_on_unset_name_fails
  - remove_nonexistent_fails       → removexattr_on_unset_name_fails

The emits live inside the `with_btrfs_rw_mount` closures, so when the
helper short-circuits (no /dev/fuse or no mkfs.btrfs) no marker is
written — a soft-skip still looks like a skip in CI artifacts, not
a false PASS.

No existing test body is modified; this is strictly diagnostic-marker
wiring.  `rch exec env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_gmi
cargo check -p ffs-harness --tests` → exit 0 (only pre-existing
FSCRYPT_POLICY_V2_SIZE warning from sibling WIP, not introduced here).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
